☠ CBI IRAQ CANARY — IQRI OIL STATE & GEOPOLITICAL INTELLIGENCE BRIEF

Central Bank of Iraq  |  IQRI: Iraq Risk Index  |  Q3 2026 Intelligence Brief
Issued: 2026-07-06  |  CivilisationOS Domain #94  |  Domain Slug: cbi_iraq_canary  |  Clerk Voice: Intelligence-Only  |  Governance: HUMAN_ONLY_MANUAL
DOMAIN 94  |  CLUSTER 5: ECONOMICS & BUSINESS  |  REGION: MIDDLE EAST
THE CANARY IS WATCHING
IQRI = 0.4250   |   Zone: WATCH   |   Currency: IQD ~1,300/USD   |   Production: ~4.2 Mbpd   |   2026-07-06T02:22:54Z

⚡ Iraq's Unique Geopolitical Position — The Country That Hosts Both the US and Iran

Iraq is the world's 2nd largest OPEC producer (~4.2 Mbpd) and sits at the Iran-US geopolitical fault line. Oil revenue constitutes 95%+ of government revenue and 85%+ of exports. The country simultaneously hosts US forces (under active political pressure to leave from Iran-aligned factions) AND Iran-backed Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF/Hashd al-Shaabi) with ~45% of parliamentary seats. Iraq cannot fully align with either the US or Iran without destroying one relationship. This dual-dependency makes Iraq the most geopolitically unique oil state in the CivilisationOS network and generates the unique KC_01 kill condition: a domestic armed actor (Kataib Hezbollah / PMF) that can physically shut down the 2nd largest OPEC producer via a single attack on Basra Oil Terminal or Rumaila field.

⚡ KC_01 SINGLE TRIGGER — ARMED & MONITORING

Trigger condition: Any confirmed PMF/Kataib Hezbollah attack on Basra Oil Terminal, Rumaila field, West Qurna-1/2, Majnoon, or the southern Iraq oil pipeline corridor immediately escalates IQRI to a minimum of 0.60 (ELEVATED), regardless of the composite score. This is Iraq's unique kill condition in the CivilisationOS network: a domestic armed actor with the capability and precedent (2019-2020 attacks) to shut down the 2nd largest OPEC producer. Current status: ARMED / NOT FIRED. Score 0.35 reflects heightened threat environment without an active confirmed attack.

IQRI Trajectory — Jan–Jul 2026

Iraq Oil Production & Fiscal Intelligence

Geopolitical Risk: PMF | US Forces | Iran Attacks | Kurdish Disputes

US Equity Impact Analysis — 3-Channel Model

CBI Iraq Canary — Master Intelligence Dashboard

◆ Intelligence Narrative — Q3 2026

IQRI BASELINE — WATCH ZONE, SINGLE TRIGGER ARMED: The Iraq Risk Index measures 0.4250 in WATCH territory as of Q3 2026. The composite reflects a structurally elevated threat environment without an active kill condition trigger. The signal reads THE CANARY IS WATCHING: conditions are monitored, no immediate escalation has materialized, but the environment is materially above NORMAL.

KC_01 — OIL INFRASTRUCTURE / PMF (0.35, weight 0.30): The dominant single-trigger condition remains the most strategically significant risk variable in the Iraq canary. Kataib Hezbollah and affiliated PMF factions have the demonstrated capability to target Basra Oil Terminal and the Rumaila field complex — as evidenced by the 2019-2020 attack cycle. Current score of 0.35 reflects a heightened threat environment: no active attack has been confirmed, but credible intelligence indicators of PMF capability and intent remain present. Any confirmed kinetic action against oil infrastructure fires the single trigger, overriding the composite IQRI to a minimum of 0.60 ELEVATED.

KC_02 — IRAN PROXY / US-IRAN TENSION (0.45, weight 0.25): The Iran influence channel scores highest among non-triggered kill conditions at 0.45. PMF political entrenchment — with ~45% of parliamentary seats via the Fatah Alliance — constrains PM Sudani's operational freedom while maintaining deniable Iran pressure on US force posture. US-Iran direct confrontation risk in Iraq remains a tail risk priced at the WATCH level. The 2023 peak of 83 Iran-backed attacks on US bases has decelerated but not ceased; the current environment reflects managed escalation rather than hot conflict.

KC_03 — OPEC+ QUOTA / OIL PRICE (0.40, weight 0.20): Iraq continues its chronic pattern of overproduction versus OPEC+ quota, currently producing ~4.2 Mbpd against a 4.0 Mbpd quota. Brent at approximately $72/bbl sits marginally above Iraq's fiscal break-even of ~$70/bbl, leaving minimal buffer. A sustained Brent decline below $70 would compress Iraq's budget to deficit territory and below $60 triggers the KC_03 scoring threshold. Iraq's inability to diversify revenue streams remains the core structural vulnerability; the fiscal system is entirely oil-dependent.

KC_04 — POLITICAL / GOVERNANCE (0.42, weight 0.15): PM Sudani's coalition government navigates ongoing pressure from Iran-aligned PMF factions demanding accelerated US troop withdrawal timelines. The Kurdish-Baghdad revenue dispute — with the KRG receiving approximately 10% of the federal budget versus the constitutional 17% claim — remains unresolved. Kurdish oil exports via Turkey's Ceyhan pipeline remain suspended following the 2023 arbitration ruling, adding pressure to Erbil-Baghdad relations. No imminent government collapse is assessed, but coalition fragility is structurally elevated.

KC_05 — BANKING / DOLLAR ACCESS (0.38, weight 0.10): The lowest-weighted kill condition nonetheless represents structural chronic stress. US Treasury AML/CTF compliance requirements imposed on CBI USD auction access in 2023 remain in effect, creating a persistent 6-8% parallel market divergence between the official IQD peg (~1,300/USD) and street rates (~1,378/USD). Iran-linked banks losing correspondent banking relationships add systemic pressure. FATF grey-listing risk for Iraq due to AML/CTF framework deficiencies continues to be monitored.

FORWARD ASSESSMENT: The CBI Iraq Canary maintains WATCH zone with the single trigger for KC_01 fully armed. The primary escalation path is a PMF/Kataib Hezbollah kinetic action against Basra Oil Terminal or Rumaila field — an event that would simultaneously trigger oil price spikes, US-Iraq diplomatic crisis, and immediate IQRI elevation to 0.60+ ELEVATED. No order recommendations are issued. Human review required before any position action.

Iraq Fiscal Breakeven Analysis

Iraq's fiscal break-even oil price is estimated at ~$70-80/bbl, reflecting committed public sector wages (~$60B/yr), PMF integration costs, and capital expenditure under the 2023-2027 development plan. At Brent $72/bbl, Iraq operates with minimal fiscal buffer.

KC_03 thresholds:
● Brent >$80: Score 0.20 (comfortable margin)
● Brent $70-$80: Score 0.35 (marginal, quota violations compound)
● Brent $60-$70: Score 0.50 (fiscal stress, emergency measures)
● Brent <$60 sustained: Score 0.75 (fiscal crisis, IMF engagement likely)

Iraq overproduction vs OPEC+ quota adds geopolitical friction — Saudi Arabia has signaled willingness to compensate by cutting own production, but cumulative OPEC+ cheating by Iraq, UAE, and Kazakhstan risks cartel credibility breakdown.

◆ US Equity Impact — 3-Channel Model

Channel 1: Oil Infrastructure (weight 0.40)

PMF/Kataib Hezbollah attack on Basra Oil Terminal or Rumaila field → immediate supply shock → Brent spike +15-30% depending on severity. Oil majors (XOM/CVX/BP/TTE/ENI) experience inverse bullish signal as their non-Iraq production re-prices at the elevated Brent benchmark. HAL/SLB experience direct operational impact from Iraq contract suspension — bearish.

Channel 2: Iran Proxy Conflict (weight 0.35)

US-Iran direct confrontation using Iraq as theater → regional war risk premium → defense stocks (RTX/LMT) inverse bullish as US Gulf deployment accelerates procurement. HAL/SLB face force majeure on all Iraq contracts, making them doubly bearish. Oil majors net neutral on this channel (price spike partially offset by operational risk).

Channel 3: OPEC Production (weight 0.25)

Iraq chronic overproduction damages oil price discipline. If OPEC+ cohesion breaks, Brent softens → XOM/CVX face mild bearish headwind on this channel alone. However, KC_01 and KC_02 channels dominate; OPEC overproduction is a secondary drag, not a primary signal.

IQRI → Impact Thresholds

NORMAL (<0.30): Oil market orderly; all channels low
WATCH (0.30-0.60): Tail risk pricing begins; oil majors mildly elevated
ELEVATED (0.60-0.80): Full channel activation; XOM/CVX/RTX/LMT inverse bullish
CRITICAL (≥0.80): Oil shock; maximum impact; HAL/SLB operational halt

Ticker Company Signal Iraq Mechanism
XOM ExxonMobil ▲ INVERSE BULLISH Oil supply shock → Brent spike → XOM earnings beat; WQ-1 stake
CVX Chevron ▲ INVERSE BULLISH Brent correlation → CVX upstream EPS uplift on Iraq disruption
BP BP ▲ INVERSE BULLISH Direct Rumaila JV; price spike > volume loss; net bullish
TTE TotalEnergies ▲ INVERSE BULLISH Halfaya field Iraq; supply disruption → Brent spike benefit
ENI Eni ▲ INVERSE BULLISH Zubair field Basra; supply shock → oil price → revenue uplift
RTX RTX (Raytheon) ▲ INVERSE BULLISH Iran-Iraq conflict → Patriot/air-defense procurement surge
LMT Lockheed Martin ▲ INVERSE BULLISH US-Iran Iraq theater → F-35/HIMARS demand; Iraq LMT contracts
HAL Halliburton ▼ BEARISH Force majeure Iraq contracts on PMF attack; ~$800M/yr revenue at risk
SLB Schlumberger (SLB) ▼ BEARISH Rumaila/Zubair services suspended under conflict; direct EPS hit

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3400  WATCH

Central Bank of Iraq operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Strait of Hormuz (critical dependency, weight 0.85); Bab-el-Mandeb (significant dependency, weight 0.40); Suez Canal (moderate dependency, weight 0.20).

Critical import dependencies include food commodities, industrial machinery, consumer electronics, refined fuels, semiconductors. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Primary exports — crude oil — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3400 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Strait of Hormuz closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Export controls on rare earth metals or semiconductors from China → critical materials shortage  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0500  NORMAL

Central Bank of Iraq is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, Iraq real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Iraq are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0500 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3200  WATCH

Central Bank of Iraq faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Drought Heatwave, Flood.

The insurance market in Iraq is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.3200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Drought Heatwave event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.3500WATCH

Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3200  WATCH

Central Bank of Iraq faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Drought Heatwave, Flood.

The insurance market in Iraq is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.3200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Drought Heatwave event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_IN01 Natural Catastrophe35%0.1500NORMAL
KC_IN02 Reinsurance Market25%0.3200WATCH
KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.3500WATCH
KC_IN04 Financial Guaranty12%0.1300NORMAL
KC_IN05 Climate Risk Dislocation8%0.2300NORMAL

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3400  WATCH

Central Bank of Iraq operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Strait of Hormuz (critical dependency, weight 0.85); Bab-el-Mandeb (significant dependency, weight 0.40); Suez Canal (moderate dependency, weight 0.20).

Critical import dependencies include food commodities, industrial machinery, consumer electronics, refined fuels, semiconductors. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Primary exports — crude oil — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3400 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Strait of Hormuz closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Export controls on rare earth metals or semiconductors from China → critical materials shortage  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_SC01 Chokepoint Disruption30%0.4200WATCH
KC_SC02 Freight Rate Stress25%0.0500NORMAL
KC_SC03 Critical Minerals20%0.3200WATCH
KC_SC04 Nearshoring / FDI Shift15%0.0500NORMAL
KC_SC05 Logistics Infrastructure10%0.1000NORMAL
ⓘ Structural config baseline — scores derived from sovereign metadata; live article analysis raises these floors.  |  Region: Middle East | Supply depth: frontier | Chokepoints: Strait of Hormuz, Suez Canal, Bab-el-Mandeb  |  Issued: 2026-07-06 08:40 UTC
Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0500  NORMAL

Central Bank of Iraq is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, Iraq real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Iraq are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0500 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal
